steps.title: Distribute the negative sign
steps.content:
Distribute the negative through the second parentheses:
$$(3x^{3}-4x+9)-(6x^{6}+8x-4) = 3x^{3}-4x+9-6x^{6}-8x+4$$

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content:
Organize by degree and combine like terms:
  • $x^{6}$ terms: $-6x^{6}$
  • $x^{3}$ terms: $3x^{3}$
  • $x$ terms: $-4x-8x=-12x$
  • Constants: $9+4=13$
$$-6x^{6}+3x^{3}-12x+13$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
